title: Retrieval Review: Pseudo-Classes and Pseudo-Elements
summary: Retrieval Review: Pseudo-Classes and Pseudo-Elements retrieves prior skills in an original changed multi-input survey state system scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css action pseudo classes, css structural pseudo classes, css form state pseudo classes, css functional pseudo classes, css pseudo elements in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed multi-input survey state system artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey state work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the survey state and selector system here. */

options.text: Changed multi-input survey state system case 1: hover and focus represent the same interaction for every user.

Changed multi-input survey state system case 1: define each state from its actual input or navigation condition, keep focus visible, and test pointer, touch, keyboard, and fragment navigation separately.
Changed multi-input survey state system case 1: put every interaction style under hover because browsers synthesize hover on all devices.
